intro: Ophelia umbellata, also known as Pygmy Pennywort or Gotu Kola, is a tropical plant native to Asia, whose leaves are used in traditional medicine. The health benefits of Ophelia umbellata include improved cognitive function, enhanced memory, and reduced inflammation, due to its anti-oxidant and neuroprotective properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include triterpenoid saponins, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. Ophelia umbellata is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, capsules, and tinctures, often in combination with other herbs, to promote overall well-being and cognitive health.
